summary: This is an advice column where Cathy O'Neil (as 'Aunt Pythia') answers reader questions on relationships, ethics, and life decisions. She critiques a TV show called Sex Box, recommends books about human sexuality, and gives frank advice on topics ranging from unhappy marriages to awkward romantic advances. The column showcases her characteristic bluntness, particularly when she tells someone in an unhappy marriage to stop being cowardly and actually communicate. She also delivers a cutting one-liner about marketing being brain manipulation when asked about Google's algorithmic power.
key_points: Waiting for your spouse to end a marriage is cowardly - if you're unhappy, say so directly rather than hoping they'll make the 'easier' choice for you
Marketing IS brain manipulation - that's literally what it is, 'turning brains into things that pay you for stuff'
When someone crosses boundaries (like hitting on you inappropriately), respond with empathy rather than judgment, but also don't encourage it - go boring and distant
Your professional reputation matters more than convenience - maintain your work's integrity even after publication
Life decisions aren't game theory - 'This is not a game, it's your life'
money_quotes: Marketing *is* manipulating brains. That's exactly what marketing is, turning brains into things that pay you for stuff.
I think that people who wait for their spouse to end a marriage are cowardly.
Fuck that. Get more selfish (if you call it that) and focus on your own unhappiness, and screw the guilt.
This is not a game, it's your life.
Tell your spouse you're unhappy, because it's more courageous, because it might be fixable, and because it will be much more likely to get fixed if you talk about it.
Some of this stuff might be pseudo-science, but I don't care, because it's about sex.
key_insight: Stop treating your life like a strategic game where you wait for optimal outcomes - be direct about what you want and need, whether in relationships, work, or understanding how corporations manipulate you.
